text: The service runs as the `orderd` user, not root. Simulate what it does: `sudo -u orderd sh -c 'echo hi >> /var/log/orderd/orderd.log'`. Does it fail the same way?
Look at who owns the directory and what its mode is: `ls -ld /var/log/orderd`. It's `root:root` and `755` — group/other only get read+execute, no write.

Give the `orderd` user write access. The cleanest fix is to make it the owner: `sudo chown orderd:orderd /var/log/orderd`. (Alternatively `sudo chmod 775` plus group membership, but ownership is simplest here.)
